title: Pelvic injuries: iliac spine avulsion (ASIS)
body_html:
Anterior superior iliac spine (ASIS) avulsion fracture

Mechanism
  • Forceful muscular contraction during sport
  • Typically seen in young athletes, avulsion through physis

Clinical features
  • Patient may hear pop or snap at time of injury
  • Weakness of hip flexion
  • Limp

Aetiology
ASIS site of attachment for:
  • sartorius (sartorius is usual culprit that avulses ASIS through the physis)
  • tensor fascia latae

Management
  • Conservative typically: crutches, analgesics, and modified activity
  • Recovery time usually 4-6 weeks.

Image sourced from Wikipedia
